Chart Support

Complete documentation for charts in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Chart Overview

The Chart class represents a chart embedded in a worksheet and provides access to chart properties and data visualization capabilities.

Chart Types

Aspose.Cells FOSS supports the following chart types:

  • 2D Charts: Bar, Column, Line, Area, Pie, Doughnut, Scatter, Bubble, Radar, Stock
  • 3D Charts: Bar3D, Column3D, Line3D, Area3D, Pie3D, Surface3D, SurfaceWireframe3D, Contour
  • Modern Chart Types: Waterfall, Treemap, Sunburst, Histogram, BoxAndWhisker, Funnel, Map

Usage Examples

Creating a Simple Chart

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add sample data
worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Month");
worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Sales");
for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++)
{
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue($"Month {i + 1}");
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(new Random().Next(1000, 5000));
}

// Create a column chart
int chartIndex = worksheet.Charts.Add(
    ChartType.Column,
    "Sheet1!$B$1:$B$6",
    5, 1, 20, 5);

Creating a Line Chart

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add data
worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("X");
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
{
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue(i * 10);
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(Math.Sin(i) * 100);
}

// Create a line chart
int chartIndex = worksheet.Charts.Add(
    ChartType.Line,
    "Sheet1!$B$1:$B$10",
    5, 1, 10, 1);

Creating a Pie Chart

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add data
worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Category");
worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Value");
worksheet.Cells["A2"].PutValue(25);
worksheet.Cells["A3"].PutValue(30);
worksheet.Cells["A4"].PutValue(45);

// Create a pie chart
int chartIndex = worksheet.Charts.Add(
    ChartType.Pie,
    "Sheet1!$B$1:$B$4",
    2, 2, 12, 2);

Chart Properties

The Chart class provides the following properties:

  • Name - Gets the chart display name
  • ChartType - Gets the chart type
  • UpperLeftRow - Zero-based row of upper-left anchor
  • UpperLeftColumn - Zero-based column of upper-left anchor
  • LowerRightRow - Zero-based row of lower-right anchor
  • LowerRightColumn - Zero-based column of lower-right anchor
  • ExtentCx - Width extent in EMU
  • ExtentCy - Height extent in EMU

ChartCollection

The ChartCollection class manages all charts in a worksheet:

  • Count - Gets the number of charts
  • this[int] - Gets chart at specified index
  • Add() - Adds a new chart of specified type
  • Parameters: chart type, data range formula, anchor coordinates

ChartType Enumeration

Complete enumeration of supported chart types:

  • 2D Types: Bar, Column, Line, Area, Pie, Doughnut, Scatter, Bubble, Radar, Stock
  • 3D Types: Bar3D, Column3D, Line3D, Area3D, Pie3D, Surface3D, SurfaceWireframe3D, Contour
  • Modern Types: Waterfall, Treemap, Sunburst, Histogram, BoxAndWhisker, Funnel, Map

Important Notes

  • Modern Chart Types: Advanced chart types (Waterfall, Treemap, etc.) must be loaded from existing XLSX files; they cannot be created programmatically using the Add() method.
  • Chart Positioning: Charts are positioned using anchor coordinates (upper-left and lower-right).
  • Data Binding: Charts reference worksheet data using formulas like "Sheet1!$A$1:$A$10".
